text.title: Anti Roadmap Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Anti Roadmap Template application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Every anti-roadmap entry MUST carry a one-sentence rationale citing a specific input (data, customer quote, constraint). 'Not now' alone is rejected.
rule.rationale: Stakeholder-management research — anchors Anti Roadmap Template rule `rationale-per-entry`.

rule.statement: Each entry MUST name a revisit trigger — either a measurable threshold ('revenue from segment ≥ $5k MRR') or a dated event ('post-launch retrospective'). Open-ended 'maybe later' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Falsifiability discipline — anchors Anti Roadmap Template rule `revisit-trigger-required`.

rule.statement: The anti-roadmap MUST be signed by the PM AND the top sponsoring stakeholder. Single-signature anti-roadmaps lack the political coverage they were designed to provide.
rule.rationale: Stakeholder governance — anchors Anti Roadmap Template rule `sponsor-sign-off`.

rule.statement: Each entry MUST carry an expiration date (≤90 days). Past-expiration entries trigger a fresh review, not silent renewal.
rule.rationale: Anti-decay practice — anchors Anti Roadmap Template rule `expiration-date`.

rule.statement: The anti-roadmap MUST be published alongside the roadmap (same page, same review cadence). Standalone anti-roadmaps are not read.
rule.rationale: Publication-discipline research — anchors Anti Roadmap Template rule `paired-with-roadmap`.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
